---
title: "Source Control"
pageId: "69ef0c339b4ca5aaa64976ba"
---

From the **Settings menu,** select **Source Control **\(see Figure 1\).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

- This page provides two integration options:
  - **GitHub**
  - **GitLab**
- Each option enables you to configure the source of your documentation repository by linking your Docuwiz workspace with the respective version control platform.
- The **Connect Now** button \(located beside each option\) initiates the setup process for the selected repository.

## **GitHub Integration**

When you click **Connect Now** under the **GitHub** section, the configuration panel expands to display input fields for connection details \(see Figure 2\).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

#### **Fields and Options**

- **Repo URL**
  - Enter the complete GitHub repository URL where your documentation files are stored.
  - The correct format should include the **username**, **repository name**, and **branch**. For example: [https://github.com/username/repository\-name/tree/branch\-name](https://github.com/username/repository-name/tree/branch-name)
  - Ensure the URL points to the **root** of your repo branch — not to a subfolder or individual file.
  - Supported documentation file formats include .json, .yaml, and .md.
- **Token**
  - Enter your **GitHub personal access token** to authenticate the connection.
  - This token ensures secure communication between Docuwiz and your GitHub repository.
- **Saving the GitHub Configurations**
  - **Save Changes: **Confirms and saves your repository configuration.
  - **Cancel: **Exits the setup process without saving any changes.
- Once connected, Docuwiz automatically pulls your repository data and synchronizes updates when changes occur in the linked branch.

## **GitLab Integration**

Similar to GitHub, you can integrate Docuwiz with **GitLab** by selecting the **Connect Now** button under the GitLab section \(see Figure 3\).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

#### **Fields and Options**

- **Repo URL**
  - Enter the full GitLab repository URL including the project name and branch.
  - Example:
  [https://gitlab.com/group\-name/project\-name/\-/tree/branch\-name](https://gitlab.com/group-name/project-name/-/tree/branch-name)
  - As with GitHub, avoid linking subfolders or individual files.
  Use only the base repository URL pointing to the branch that stores your documentation files.
- **Token**
  - Provide your **GitLab personal access token** to authenticate and establish a secure connection.
  - This ensures Docuwiz can fetch, sync, and update documentation content directly from your GitLab repository.
- **Saving the GitLab Configurations**
  - **Save Changes: **Saves the entered GitLab configuration and connects your workspace to the repository.
  - **Cancel: **Cancels the operation without applying any modifications.
- Once connected to either GitHub or GitLab, Docuwiz automatically imports documentation files \(.yaml, .json, .md\). 

***Learn more about Connecting and Syncing with Source Control →***

>
